mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) captures consumer demand strength that flows directly into corporate revenue; higher RSAFS momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive RSAFS momentum months do not exceed baseline (t-test p > 0.10) or the rank correlation between RSAFS change and SPX returns is below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points within a three-month window signals deteriorating labor income and consumption expectations, causing equity risk premia to expand and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances, or the mean 60-day return during triggered months is not significantly lower than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held below 0.5 percent, month-over-month increases in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) generate negative real interest rates that drive capital reallocation from bonds to equities; the interaction of low FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The conditional marginal effect of CPIAUCSL on 20-day SPX returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% is not positive and statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term adds no incremental R-squared (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) predicts positive S&P 500 returns only during the early phase of an economic recovery when capacity utilization is low and margin expansion is largest; outside this phase the signal is weak.
failure_condition: In periods labeled post-recession recovery, positive INDPRO momentum does not coincide with positive 5-day SPX returns in at least 60% of instances, or the Information Coefficient in this regime falls below 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1,100 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1,100, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is positive in more than 55% of observations, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) confirm that income growth is translating into spending rather than being saved; this joint signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of coincident PAYEMS and RSAFS increases yields positive 5-day SPX returns in fewer than 60% of instances over a 2-year rolling window, or the rank correlation of the interaction score with 5-day returns is below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: A declining federal funds rate over a three-month window reduces short-term borrowing costs and improves corporate net interest margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following 3-month FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than returns following flat or rising FEDFUNDS (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth (GDPC1) above 2.0 percent annualized signals robust aggregate demand that reduces recession probability and compresses equity risk premia, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered quarters (GDPC1 > 2.0%) do not produce positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 65% of cases, or the mean return in triggered quarters is not significantly above the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10).
